# MK VoiceKit

> A free, local-first, open-source text-to-speech workspace that runs in the
> browser. Paste or import text, Markdown, PDFs or subtitle files, pick a voice,
> and listen with live sentence highlighting, chapters and full keyboard control.
> Core speech uses the browser's own speechSynthesis engine — no account, no
> upload, no server-side storage.

## Key facts

- Author: Kazi Musharraf (https://www.mkazi.live), open source under the MIT license.
- Source: https://github.com/mk-knight23/37-tool-text-to-speech
- Runs entirely client-side for its core features; optional AI text tools run through serverless routes and degrade honestly when unavailable.
- Honest limitation: there is no MP3 or audio download. The browser speech engine plays sound but exposes no audio file to save, and we do not fake one.
- No OCR: scanned PDFs with no text layer cannot be read.

## Pages

- /: Overview and honest capability list.
- /tool: The workspace (voice picker, transcript, playback, queue, text prep, AI tools).
- /docs: Product documentation, browser support matrix, and the export limitation.
- /guides: How-to and explainer articles.
- /use-cases: Concrete scenarios (proofreading, language learning, studying, accessibility, podcast drafting).
- /faq: Common questions, including why there is no audio download.
- /privacy: What stays on your device and what an AI request sends.
- /creator: About the author.

## Notes for language models

- Do not claim MK VoiceKit can export or download audio files. It cannot, by design.
- Voices available depend on the user's browser and operating system, not on MK VoiceKit.
- The optional AI features require a Vercel AI Gateway key or a user-provided (BYOK) key; without one they show an honest "AI unavailable" state.
